response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack/web engineer with strong software craftsmanship (TDD, CI/CD) and some low-latency systems experience, but the resume shows no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU or AI-accelerator architecture, performance analysis of deep-learning workloads, or hardware-software co-design — the core requirements of a Principal Performance Architect role. While the BS plus 15+ years of experience satisfies the educational path, the domain mismatch is fundamental rather than incremental.
strengths: 15+ years of professional software engineering experience, meeting the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, open-source contributions, testing frameworks, and community teaching
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience anywhere in the resume
No deep-learning workload, GPU, SIMD/MIMD, or AI-accelerator performance experience
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture background
No AI/deep-learning workload or GPU performance experience
Solid systems-level and low-latency software experience
